chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمَةِ أَنْ تَلْبَسَهُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ الْحُلِيِّ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهَا مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 85 – What is allowed for the female in Ihraam to wear from the clothes, and the ornaments, and what is disliked for her from that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/292)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/292)
text: الْمُحْرِمَةُ لَا تَتَنَقَّبُ لِأَنَّ إِحْرَامَ الْمَرْأَةِ فِي وَجْهِهَا وَ إِحْرَامَ الرَّجُلِ فِي رَأْسِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Abdullah Bin Maymoun,

(It has been narrated) from Ja’farasws, from hisasws fatherasws having said: ‘The female in Ihraam cannot wear Naqaab, because the Ihraam of the woman is in her face, and an Ihraam of the man is in his head’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from ‘Abd Allah ibn Maymun who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has narrated from his father, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, who has said that a woman in the state of Ihram must not use veil over her face because Ihram of women is for her face and for men it is for their head.’”
